page: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is a virtual server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD processor with 192 vCPUs and 192 physical cores, operating with 1.0 thread per core. The CPU resources are allocated on a shared basis. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of memory, which translates to a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration does not include any local GPU accelerators, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or direct GPU-based machine learning workloads. Storage is provided via 1 GB of network-attached storage, indicating that primary storage needs must be met through external network volumes. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the shared CPU allocation model, this instance offers a high density of virtual cores and memory at a qualitative cost structure typical of shared-resource environments. This resource density is suitable for highly parallelized workloads that can tolerate shared CPU performance, such as large-scale web serving, general-purpose application hosting, distributed testing environments, and memory-intensive tasks that do not require dedicated, continuous CPU execution.
description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is an x86_64 virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 192 AMD vCPUs and 192 physical cores on a shared CPU allocation model. It is configured with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 1 GB of network-attached storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address, with no integrated GPU accelerators. Due to its shared CPU architecture and high core count, this server offers a cost-efficient option for highly parallelized, memory-intensive workloads that do not demand dedicated CPU performance. It is suited for large-scale web serving, general-purpose application hosting, and distributed testing environments.
